syntax_rules.formatting: Strictly plain text raw Mermaid syntax inside 'mermaid_code'. No markdown fences (like ```mermaid) inside JSON values.
syntax_rules.strict_identifier_preservation: CRITICAL: You MUST use the exact original 'identifier' from the parsed JSON elements (e.g., 'US-01', 'FR-001', 'ENT-USER'). NEVER shorten or alter them to 'US1', 'FR1', or 'E_USER'.

er_diagram.source_data: Extract from 'elements' with type ENTITY, DATABASE, or MODEL and their 'relations'.
er_diagram.rule: Generate erDiagram using entity attributes and relational constraints (PK/FK). Use exact entity identifiers or entity names.

sequence_diagram.source_data: Extract from 'elements' (Endpoints/APIs), 'user_stories', and user/system interaction flows.
sequence_diagram.rule: Mandatory when user stories or API endpoints involve multi-step actor-system interactions.

flowchart_traceability.source_data: Extract from 'elements' (Requirements, User Stories, Tasks) and structural 'sections'.
flowchart_traceability.rule: Model requirements traceability using EXACT original identifiers (e.g., US-01, FR-001) as node IDs. Must include subgraphs and explicit full-text labels.

workflow_complexity.source_data: Extract business logic, conditions, and task phases.
workflow_complexity.rule: Purely linear flowcharts are FORBIDDEN. Must include at least one decision diamond '{?}' and loop or alternative branch.

diagrams.title: Explicit title of the diagram
diagrams.type: Must be one of the allowed_types (e.g., flowchart, sequenceDiagram, erDiagram)
diagrams.description: Brief operational context based on extracted elements or sections
diagrams.mermaid_code: Valid raw Mermaid.js syntax starting strictly with diagram type header
